item.title: I would buy this if I could.
item.content:encoded: This seems really fun, so fun it should be a Wondertainment product. Something like "Self growing garden! Watch the little gnome do it's work, as you grow a garden anywhere you like! Watch garden decorations as they come to life! Look in awe as they build their own little community! New self-growing garden, by Dr. Wondertainment."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe it's Euclid because the gnomes that appear have free will, and can therefore attempt a containment breach.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ show block
– hide block

Heh, this is funny. When I suggested to the other team members, Roget was reminded of his story of coming up with creating SCP-1507. He ALSO almost made 1507 into garden gnomes, so I think it fits.

I propose the following edits:

Pink plastic flamingos. It is noted that instead of legs, this specimen has one thin pole

to this:

Pink plastic flamingos. It is noted that instead of legs, this specimen has one thin pole

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Its a very interesting concept, but in the beginning of the Description part i feel like the word "It" is used way too much, making the text sound strange/repetitive. One of these sentences could easily be replaced with " SCP-1054"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not bad. It takes that cliche of "It has an effect that slowly spreads," but since it's safe, it feels different from that cliche. It does have some charm to it, and it takes an object which has not been used before.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image in this article was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact anqxyr or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, this SCP is rather cute and has a very certain charm to it. In fact, it is my favorite non-threatening SCP out of all the ones I've read. Maybe it has something to do with my obsession with gnomes, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just going to say I love the precision in the item description.
"a sack of unknown contents" and "dance-like position" are great tone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. I do find myself wondering, at the end of it, whether the creatures created remain (and whether they remain active) when the gnome is lifted from the ground and growth is ceased. If so, it should be noted. If not, I would expect that the Foundation would have either an inventory of creatures being stored or instructions on safe disposal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. Maybe it's just me being paranoid, but think of the posible consequences of their religious behavior. Would they attack someone who tries to take SCP-1054 away from them?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …If you let it get big enough, would the "yard" sprout sea serpents? Because that would rock.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not upping yet

Chuggs, I think there's a couple tweaks to the grammar you could use, and the article seems a little blah, but I BELIEVE IN YOU

It's very cute though. I think Euclid might have been appropriate, because materials grow out of the ground to build little civilizations. Who knows what those dudes could make?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting read, good job.

On a side note. Am I the only one who wants an experiment log involving SPC-963? Dr. Bright- The Gnome God-king!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Incidentally, it would be better if the Digging Dogs are just the back halves of dogs. Because that's all that protrudes from the ground.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is my thought, too, and I find it ironic that the first comments were asking why it was a Euclid.

I could see this being Safe only if it needs to 'infect' a certain area before it can produce SCP-1054-1; that keeping it in this steel box just means there's 9m2 of astroturf in there. There are a few things that still need to be done with this one as it feels unfinished, but the whimsicality alone makes it good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bloody hell, you two. Those ideas are brilliant. I'll start writing that up now!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1
Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, it's just the right middle ground between "I want this" and "I want to know what else it does".

If it's left on real grass like you'd expect, does the astroturf replace the grass, or just grow within it and you end up with a half-plastic lawn?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one. It gives me that feeling of "this is interesting, I want to find out more about it." +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The air turns to grass.

Maybe you should clarify that the surface has to be in a solid state.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if they just hung it from a string in the box?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is definitely Euclid. If we put it in a locked box and leave it alone, eventually the locked box will burst open from the quantity of astroturf that has accumulated inside.

We have to move this thing to a new location every 60 minutes or it starts creating new matter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the concept but I think your article needs more information, like what happens to SCP-1054-1's when removed from astro turf and maybe researchers attempting communication with gnomes. Your article could also use some tightening up.

For example:

"The steel door that is used to access the cell should be airtight," I don't think the reader will mistake the steel door for any other steel doors so you could just say "The steel door should be airtight." Also 'should' implies that this is a suggestion and not an order "The steel door must be airtight."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me of the unexplained playful nature that minecraft does to me. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That would explain why it's an SCP and not just an Anomalous Item. It's cool as is, but not really something that needs SCP-level containment, you know? But if everything involved became a part of a self-contained ecosystem (the Birds and Flamingos feed on the astroturf, and in turn provide food for the gnomes; the Dogs drop novelty doggie-doo, which fertilizes the astroturf and increases its rate of spread)…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are Play-Doh warriors and moving Legos, why not a garden gnone that creates its' own worshippers? Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. Could use some expanding like test logs or more explanation on the -1s and the ritual the gnomes perform. Upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually like this a lot; that said, I do have a thing for quirky-rather-than-scary SCPs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A cool hook would be a whole Garden-based ecosystem. The gnomes could utilize the other life forms in a variety of ways, such as catching the Fountain Birds and shaving the Dogs for Fur.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That movie was a disservice to all garden dwelling statuary. this SCP is taking it back! Upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can buy the former Euclid designation. Given enough time and space to grow who knows what will pop out of that lawn eventually? It spawned a little tribe of worshipers at one point. But it seems safe enough to contain in a locked box so Safe is good enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: aww thats a shame, you just reminded me that existed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminds me a LOT of "Gnomeo and Juliet". No vote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite like it, having something light hearted is a pleasant change. I don't think just because it is not sinister that it should be a -J SCP, either. However, would this be more of anomalous object? Does fake grass growing take over grass outside, killing natural life forms if put outdoors? Do the "life forms" attempt to communicate? I would like to know!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to like this, but it's just not interesting enough yet. Will keep an eye on any edits.

EDIT: Not bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was Euclid before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP is oddly cute, and quite un-threatening. I certainly wouldn't want many entries like it, but as the odd one off, it makes a nice change. Upvoted, but some small grammar and tone issues should be fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As I said in chat, I like it. Not every SCP has to be scary to work. This one's cute and whimsical.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It doesn't need to be immediatly harmful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For what it is worth, I like this. It seems fun and unusal though not overly dangerous. I think a few test logs would be fun. I thought it's nice, though it may more likely be safe, or even anomalous.
Upvoted :).

Edit: I reread it and I like it even more, I hope it sticks around. Though glad its safe now. nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I second that. Doesn't seem that harmful at all. In fact, I think it should be a -J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is it Euclid?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: New SCP. My first!
